name: Magical School Bus
service_types: Group Family Day Care (NOT school bus / not student transportation)
districts_or_customers: Licensed home daycare, Bronx NY, capacity 16 children
recent_news: Operating as a NYS OCFS-licensed Group Family Day Care since 7/13/2012; no transportation operations identified.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Despite the name, Magical School Bus is a home-based Group Family Day Care (daycare) in the Bronx, not a school-bus contractor. Exclude from Summit pipeline.

name: R Us School Transportation Inc
fleet_size_est: unknown; ~19 employees suggests small fleet (est. 5-10 vehicles)
service_types: School transportation (presumed private school bus contractor, Miami-Dade)
districts_or_customers: Likely Miami-Dade area private schools; not found on M-DCPS approved private bus company list reviewed
recent_news: No public news, M&A, or regulatory items located. No company website confirmed.
sources: https://www.miamidade.gov/global/license.page?Mduid_license=lic163353226680290; M-DCPS approved private bus companies list (Taty/R Us not present in reviewed copy)
fit_notes: Very small Miami-area operator with minimal digital footprint. Sub-scale (~$845K revenue), too small to be a stand-alone tuck-in; could be a roll-up cluster candidate if Summit is building Miami-Dade density, but standalone fit is low.

name: Taty School Bus
principals: Tatiana M. Ross (Owner / Registered Agent / Manager)
fleet_size_est: very small (est. 2-5 vehicles based on 6 employees and small-tier classification)
service_types: Door-to-door school transportation, school excursions, summer camps; routes to Hialeah Gardens Elementary, Middle, and Senior High
districts_or_customers: Private/parent-pay routes serving Hialeah Gardens and West Hialeah schools (Hialeah Gardens Elementary/Middle/Senior High); Miami-Dade County
recent_news: DBA Tatiana Ross active since 2010 (FMCSA), Florida LLC re-formed 7/11/2023; family business marketed as 20+ years of school transportation experience; no M&A or ownership change identified.
fit_notes: Independent, family-run, single-owner Miami-Dade operator -- classic tuck-in profile but at micro scale (~$223K revenue, 6 employees). Word-of-mouth parent-pay book; 25+ year owner tenure suggests succession potential. Useful only as a small bolt-on to a larger South Florida platform footprint.

name: Campus Transport LLC
fleet_size_est: unknown; 24 employees implies small fleet (~5-10 vehicles)
service_types: Transportation (school transport per registration); a separately filed Sasha Salerno DBA 'Campus Auto Retail Service' was identified as an inactive FMCSA authority
districts_or_customers: Possibly Volusia County / DeLand area; not confirmed as a Volusia County Schools contractor in publicly reviewed sources
recent_news: No news, website, or M&A activity located. Related Salerno trucking authority listed as inactive at FMCSA.
fit_notes: Thin public footprint, very small (~$934K revenue), uncertain whether it operates yellow school buses or shuttle/transport service. Not a meaningful standalone tuck-in for Summit; would require direct outreach to validate fleet and contracts.

name: Miller Motor Co
owner_or_parent: Mlaker Student Transportation (Davidsville, PA) -- acquired summer 2024
principals: Dave Miller (former 3rd-gen owner, business sold); founders Bernie Miller (grandfather) and Frank Miller (father); Ken Shreve listed as manager in older records
fleet_size_est: unknown specifically; Mlaker now transports 8,500+ students/day regionwide
service_types: School bus contracting (yellow bus, K-12 home-to-school)
districts_or_customers: Richland SD, Forest Hills SD, and Windber Area SD (Cambria/Somerset counties, PA)
recent_news: Summer 2024: Dave Miller sold Miller Motor Co to longtime partner Mlaker Student Transportation; Mlaker assumed operations, retained all employees, will rebrand fleet over time (Tribune-Democrat, Aug 2024).
fit_notes: NOT an available tuck-in -- already acquired by Mlaker Student Transportation in summer 2024. Mlaker itself (third-generation family operator, 8,500+ daily riders in western PA) is the more interesting prospective Summit target; Miller Motor should be retired from the pipeline.
